title: Better pull error for fully-qualified images
body:
When pulling a fully-qualified image that fails, we should not be talking about
registries/search registries in the the error message as it is not applicable.  If
a image that is fq'd and fails to pull, the error should be simplified.

```
$ sudo podman pull this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo
Trying to pull this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo...Failed
error pulling image "this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo": unable to pull this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo: unable to pull image, or you do not have pull access
$
```

Resolves: #1212
Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM, though we really need to look into getting the error strings from c/image out to the user - we are going to need them for debugging at some point
Correct, I think we got to allow @mtrmac to work through a common solution for Buildah and Podman, and I guess eventually CRI-O.

:pushpin: Commit de023c9 has been approved by `mheon`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mheon de023c980aecc55becfd61a3cf028942f8a7851b -->
:hourglass: Testing commit de023c9 with merge ca2d2d4...

As an FYI, @mtrmac put together a quick first pass at:  https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/909.  Here's the issue that started the discussion at: https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/849
@mheon, @baude I think it's best if we hold off merging this for a few days until we let @mtrmac attack the c/i errors.

@TomSweeneyRedHat We aren't actually even printing c/image errors right now... So this is basically independent of that work. Once the c/image errors work happens, we can print the error for each pull on a given name.
However, I am going to reopen #1212 if this closes it, because we need the c/image error work to really resolve this

Approved by: mheon
Pushing ca2d2d4d1dace9bb142c0b0d36b62b163f9aa1f3 to master...
:eyes: Test was successful, but fast-forwarding failed: 422 Update is not a fast forward
Well, we have a reprieve for re-review after Homu barfed.
(ACK on the general idea, sure.  Ultimately c/image errors, or some better version of them, should be reported, but this is a clear localized improvement.)
